headlines.title: Court orders Trump administration to rehire fired intelligence officers
headlines.description: The administration fired 19 officers of the CIA and the National Intelligence Director’s Office who had been assigned to jobs promoting diversity.

headlines.title: Iran and Oman propose fee plan for Strait of Hormuz, sources say
headlines.description: Monetizing the strait would be a dramatic change from before the war, when cargo ships transited without paying any fee.

headlines.title: Air Force officer arrested at Capitol after calling for Trump’s impeachment
headlines.description: Maj. Jason Watson was in uniform when U.S. Capitol Police arrested him. He criticized Trump’s actions in Iran and Venezuela, as well as his immigration policies.

headlines.title: Paramedics responded to a ‘cardiac arrest’ at Mitch McConnell’s home on day of hospitalization
headlines.description: McConnell’s office said Thursday that he is still in the hospital. It hasn’t disclosed why the Kentucky Republican was admitted on June 14.

headlines.title: Paramedics responded to a ‘cardiac arrest’ at Mitch McConnell’s home on day of hospitalization
headlines.description: Paramedics conducted CPR on an individual experiencing a “cardiac arrest” at a known address for Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) on the day his office disclosed he had been hospitalized, according to police scanner audio obtained by NBC News. Sen. McConnell remains at the hospital, according to a spokesperson. NBC News' Sahil Kapur reports.

headlines.title: Pirro announces charges against David Hearn for allegedly vandalizing Reflecting Pool
headlines.description: The U.S. attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro announced charges against Olympic canoeist David “Davey” Hearn in connection with the alleged vandalism at the Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C.

headlines.title: 'Everybody should be able to participate’: Advocate for open primaries
headlines.description: As midterms approach, advocates of election reform warn Congress could soon become more polarized. John Opdycke, Founder and President of Open Primaries, joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss how closed, single-party primaries exclude independent voters and could feed polarization in U.S. politics.

headlines.title: As the nation turns 250, many Americans say flying the Stars and Stripes is now a red flag
headlines.description: The American flag can have many meanings, whether hung on front porches or waved in parades and protests. To some, it’s also tied up with President Trump.

headlines.title: America at 250 is riven with doubt and pessimism — but with glimmers of hope
headlines.description: Americans have a bleak outlook on the nation’s future ahead of its 250th birthday next month, with most saying the U.S. has already seen its best days and a record-low number saying they are extremely proud to be Americans, according to a new NBC News poll

headlines.title: Great American State Fair attendees say they haven’t noticed politics overshadowing the event
headlines.description: Trump kicked off the event with a headlining speech Wednesday after musical acts dropped out of the lineup citing concerns over partisanship.

headlines.title: Americans believe they aren’t being taught enough about democracy
headlines.description: For Thomas Jefferson, James Madison and other bold-face names struggling to bring about a new nation, an educated and informed citizenry was essential to the success of the American experiment.

headlines.title: Americans see a huge divide between haves and have-nots, new poll shows
headlines.description: A national NBC News poll finds 81% of Americans say there is more that divides than unites the wealthy and ordinary working people.
